title: Zero-State Response and Causality
summary: The page states the width property of convolution: if two signals have finite durations T1 and T2, then their convolution has duration T1 + T2. It then derives the convolution integral for causal input and impulse response, showing that the zero-state response of a causal LTIC system is also causal and reducing the integral limits accordingly.

figures.caption: Width property of convolution showing x1(t), x2(t), and x1(t) * x2(t) with widths T1, T2, and T1 + T2
